framework.name: NIST Cybersecurity Framework (CSF) 2.0
framework.description: The NIST Cybersecurity Framework 2.0 organizes cybersecurity risk management outcomes into six Functions (Govern, Identify, Protect, Detect, Respond, Recover), 22 Categories, and 106 Subcategories. Each Subcategory is captured here as a requirement.

requirements.name: GV.OC-01 — Organizational mission understood
requirements.description: The organization's mission is understood and used to inform how cybersecurity risk is managed.

requirements.name: GV.OC-02 — Stakeholder expectations understood
requirements.description: The needs and expectations of internal and external stakeholders regarding cybersecurity are identified and considered.

requirements.name: GV.OC-03 — Legal and regulatory requirements
requirements.description: Applicable legal, regulatory, and contractual cybersecurity obligations, including privacy, are understood and managed.

requirements.name: GV.OC-04 — Critical objectives and services
requirements.description: The critical capabilities, services, and objectives that stakeholders expect are identified and communicated.

requirements.name: GV.OC-05 — External dependencies understood
requirements.description: The external outcomes, capabilities, and services the organization relies on are understood and communicated.

requirements.name: GV.RM-01 — Risk management objectives agreed
requirements.description: Cybersecurity risk management objectives are established and agreed upon by organizational stakeholders.

requirements.name: GV.RM-02 — Risk appetite and tolerance
requirements.description: Statements of risk appetite and risk tolerance are defined, communicated, and kept current.

requirements.name: GV.RM-03 — Risk integrated into ERM
requirements.description: Cybersecurity risk activities and outcomes are incorporated into enterprise risk management processes.

requirements.name: GV.RM-04 — Risk response strategy set
requirements.description: A strategic direction outlining appropriate risk response options is defined and communicated.

requirements.name: GV.RM-05 — Risk communication channels
requirements.description: Communication lines for cybersecurity risk, including third-party risk, are established across the organization.

requirements.name: GV.RM-06 — Standardized risk method
requirements.description: A consistent method for calculating, documenting, categorizing, and prioritizing cyber risk is established.

requirements.name: GV.RM-07 — Strategic opportunities considered
requirements.description: Positive risks or strategic opportunities are characterized and included in cybersecurity risk discussions.

requirements.name: GV.RR-01 — Leadership accountability and culture
requirements.description: Leadership is accountable for cyber risk and fosters a risk-aware, ethical, continually improving culture.

requirements.name: GV.RR-02 — Roles and authorities enforced
requirements.description: Cybersecurity roles, responsibilities, and authorities are defined, communicated, understood, and enforced.

requirements.name: GV.RR-03 — Resources allocated to risk
requirements.description: Resources are allocated in proportion to the cybersecurity strategy, roles, responsibilities, and policies.

requirements.name: GV.RR-04 — Cybersecurity in HR practices
requirements.description: Cybersecurity considerations are embedded into human resources practices.

requirements.name: GV.PO-01 — Cybersecurity policy established
requirements.description: A cybersecurity risk management policy is established from organizational context and communicated and enforced.

requirements.name: GV.PO-02 — Policy reviewed and updated
requirements.description: The cybersecurity policy is reviewed and updated to reflect changes in requirements, threats, and mission.

requirements.name: GV.OV-01 — Strategy outcomes reviewed
requirements.description: Outcomes of the cyber risk strategy are reviewed to inform and adjust the strategy and direction.

requirements.name: GV.OV-02 — Strategy coverage reviewed
requirements.description: The risk management strategy is reviewed and adjusted to cover organizational requirements and risks.

requirements.name: GV.OV-03 — Risk performance evaluated
requirements.description: Cybersecurity risk management performance is measured and reviewed to identify needed adjustments.

requirements.name: GV.SC-01 — C-SCRM program established
requirements.description: A cybersecurity supply chain risk management program and its processes are established and agreed upon.

requirements.name: GV.SC-02 — Supplier roles coordinated
requirements.description: Cyber roles and responsibilities for suppliers, customers, and partners are established and coordinated.

requirements.name: GV.SC-03 — C-SCRM integrated into ERM
requirements.description: Supply chain risk management is integrated into cyber and enterprise risk and improvement processes.

requirements.name: GV.SC-04 — Suppliers prioritized by criticality
requirements.description: Suppliers are identified and prioritized according to their criticality.

requirements.name: GV.SC-05 — Supply chain requirements in contracts
requirements.description: Requirements addressing supply chain cyber risk are prioritized and built into supplier agreements.

requirements.name: GV.SC-06 — Due diligence before engagement
requirements.description: Planning and due diligence are performed to reduce risk before forming third-party relationships.

requirements.name: GV.SC-07 — Third-party risk monitored
requirements.description: Risks from suppliers and third parties are assessed, prioritized, responded to, and monitored over time.

requirements.name: GV.SC-08 — Suppliers in incident response
requirements.description: Relevant suppliers and third parties are included in incident planning, response, and recovery.

requirements.name: GV.SC-09 — Supply chain security in lifecycle
requirements.description: Supply chain security practices are integrated and monitored across the product and service life cycle.

requirements.name: GV.SC-10 — Post-relationship provisions
requirements.description: Supply chain risk plans address activities occurring after a partnership or agreement concludes.

requirements.name: ID.AM-01 — Hardware inventory maintained
requirements.description: Inventories of hardware managed by the organization are maintained.

requirements.name: ID.AM-02 — Software and services inventory
requirements.description: Inventories of organization-managed software, services, and systems are maintained.

requirements.name: ID.AM-03 — Network data flows mapped
requirements.description: Representations of authorized network communications and internal and external data flows are maintained.

requirements.name: ID.AM-04 — Supplier services inventory
requirements.description: Inventories of services provided by suppliers are maintained.

requirements.name: ID.AM-05 — Assets prioritized
requirements.description: Assets are prioritized based on classification, criticality, resources, and mission impact.

requirements.name: ID.AM-07 — Data inventories maintained
requirements.description: Inventories of data and corresponding metadata for designated data types are maintained.

requirements.name: ID.AM-08 — Assets managed across lifecycle
requirements.description: Systems, hardware, software, services, and data are managed throughout their life cycles.

requirements.name: ID.RA-01 — Vulnerabilities identified
requirements.description: Vulnerabilities in assets are identified, validated, and recorded.

requirements.name: ID.RA-02 — Threat intelligence received
requirements.description: Cyber threat intelligence is obtained from information sharing forums and sources.

requirements.name: ID.RA-03 — Threats identified
requirements.description: Internal and external threats to the organization are identified and recorded.

requirements.name: ID.RA-04 — Impacts and likelihoods identified
requirements.description: Potential impacts and likelihoods of threats exploiting vulnerabilities are identified and recorded.

requirements.name: ID.RA-05 — Risk understood and prioritized
requirements.description: Threats, vulnerabilities, likelihoods, and impacts are used to determine risk and prioritize response.

requirements.name: ID.RA-06 — Risk responses chosen
requirements.description: Risk responses are selected, prioritized, planned, tracked, and communicated.

requirements.name: ID.RA-07 — Changes and exceptions managed
requirements.description: Changes and exceptions are managed, assessed for risk impact, recorded, and tracked.

requirements.name: ID.RA-08 — Vulnerability disclosures handled
requirements.description: Processes for receiving, analyzing, and responding to vulnerability disclosures are established.

requirements.name: ID.RA-09 — Hardware/software integrity assessed
requirements.description: Authenticity and integrity of hardware and software are assessed before acquisition and use.

requirements.name: ID.RA-10 — Critical suppliers assessed
requirements.description: Critical suppliers are assessed prior to acquisition.

requirements.name: ID.IM-01 — Improvements from evaluations
requirements.description: Improvements are identified from evaluations of the cybersecurity program.

requirements.name: ID.IM-02 — Improvements from tests
requirements.description: Improvements are identified from security tests and exercises, including those with third parties.

requirements.name: ID.IM-03 — Improvements from operations
requirements.description: Improvements are identified from executing operational processes, procedures, and activities.

requirements.name: ID.IM-04 — Response plans maintained
requirements.description: Incident response and other cybersecurity plans are established, communicated, maintained, and improved.

requirements.name: PR.AA-01 — Identities and credentials managed
requirements.category: Identity Management, Authentication, and Access Control
requirements.description: Identities and credentials for authorized users, services, and hardware are managed.

requirements.name: PR.AA-02 — Identities proofed and bound
requirements.category: Identity Management, Authentication, and Access Control
requirements.description: Identities are proofed and bound to credentials based on the context of interactions.

requirements.name: PR.AA-03 — Authentication performed
requirements.category: Identity Management, Authentication, and Access Control
requirements.description: Users, services, and hardware are authenticated.

requirements.name: PR.AA-04 — Identity assertions protected
requirements.category: Identity Management, Authentication, and Access Control
requirements.description: Identity assertions are protected, conveyed, and verified.

requirements.name: PR.AA-05 — Access enforced with least privilege
requirements.category: Identity Management, Authentication, and Access Control
requirements.description: Access permissions are defined, enforced, and reviewed using least privilege and separation of duties.

requirements.name: PR.AA-06 — Physical access managed
requirements.category: Identity Management, Authentication, and Access Control
requirements.description: Physical access to assets is managed, monitored, and enforced commensurate with risk.

requirements.name: PR.AT-01 — General awareness training
requirements.description: Personnel receive awareness and training to handle general tasks with cybersecurity risk in mind.

requirements.name: PR.AT-02 — Specialized role training
requirements.description: Individuals in specialized roles receive training to perform their tasks with cybersecurity risk in mind.

requirements.name: PR.DS-01 — Data-at-rest protected
requirements.description: The confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data at rest are protected.

requirements.name: PR.DS-02 — Data-in-transit protected
requirements.description: The confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data in transit are protected.

requirements.name: PR.DS-10 — Data-in-use protected
requirements.description: The confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data in use are protected.

requirements.name: PR.DS-11 — Backups created and tested
requirements.description: Backups of data are created, protected, maintained, and tested.

requirements.name: PR.PS-01 — Configuration management
requirements.description: Configuration management practices are established and applied.

requirements.name: PR.PS-02 — Software maintenance
requirements.description: Software is maintained, replaced, and removed commensurate with risk.

requirements.name: PR.PS-03 — Hardware maintenance
requirements.description: Hardware is maintained, replaced, and removed commensurate with risk.

requirements.name: PR.PS-04 — Log records generated
requirements.description: Log records are generated and made available for continuous monitoring.

requirements.name: PR.PS-05 — Unauthorized software prevented
requirements.description: Installation and execution of unauthorized software are prevented.

requirements.name: PR.PS-06 — Secure software development
requirements.description: Secure software development practices are integrated and monitored throughout the SDLC.

requirements.name: PR.IR-01 — Networks protected
requirements.description: Networks and environments are protected from unauthorized logical access and usage.

requirements.name: PR.IR-02 — Assets protected from environment
requirements.description: Technology assets are protected from environmental threats.

requirements.name: PR.IR-03 — Resilience mechanisms implemented
requirements.description: Mechanisms are implemented to meet resilience requirements in normal and adverse situations.

requirements.name: PR.IR-04 — Resource capacity maintained
requirements.description: Adequate resource capacity is maintained to ensure availability.

requirements.name: DE.CM-01 — Networks monitored
requirements.description: Networks and network services are monitored to find potentially adverse events.

requirements.name: DE.CM-02 — Physical environment monitored
requirements.description: The physical environment is monitored to find potentially adverse events.

requirements.name: DE.CM-03 — Personnel activity monitored
requirements.description: Personnel activity and technology usage are monitored to find potentially adverse events.

requirements.name: DE.CM-06 — External providers monitored
requirements.description: External service provider activities and services are monitored to find potentially adverse events.

requirements.name: DE.CM-09 — Computing assets monitored
requirements.description: Computing hardware, software, runtime environments, and data are monitored for adverse events.

requirements.name: DE.AE-02 — Adverse events analyzed
requirements.description: Potentially adverse events are analyzed to better understand the associated activity.

requirements.name: DE.AE-03 — Information correlated
requirements.description: Event information is correlated from multiple sources.

requirements.name: DE.AE-04 — Impact and scope understood
requirements.description: The estimated impact and scope of adverse events are determined.

requirements.name: DE.AE-06 — Event information shared
requirements.description: Information about adverse events is provided to authorized staff and tools.

requirements.name: DE.AE-07 — Threat intel integrated
requirements.description: Cyber threat intelligence and contextual information are integrated into event analysis.

requirements.name: DE.AE-08 — Incidents declared
requirements.description: Incidents are declared when adverse events meet the defined incident criteria.

requirements.name: RS.MA-01 — Response plan executed
requirements.description: The incident response plan is executed with relevant third parties once an incident is declared.

requirements.name: RS.MA-02 — Reports triaged
requirements.description: Incident reports are triaged and validated.

requirements.name: RS.MA-03 — Incidents prioritized
requirements.description: Incidents are categorized and prioritized.

requirements.name: RS.MA-04 — Incidents escalated
requirements.description: Incidents are escalated or elevated as needed.

requirements.name: RS.MA-05 — Recovery criteria applied
requirements.description: The criteria for initiating incident recovery are applied.

requirements.name: RS.AN-03 — Incident analysis performed
requirements.description: Analysis determines what occurred during an incident and identifies its root cause.

requirements.name: RS.AN-06 — Investigation actions recorded
requirements.description: Investigation actions are recorded with their integrity and provenance preserved.

requirements.name: RS.AN-07 — Incident data preserved
requirements.description: Incident data and metadata are collected with their integrity and provenance preserved.

requirements.name: RS.AN-08 — Incident magnitude estimated
requirements.description: An incident's magnitude is estimated and validated.

requirements.name: RS.CO-02 — Stakeholders notified
requirements.description: Internal and external stakeholders are notified of incidents.

requirements.name: RS.CO-03 — Information shared
requirements.description: Information is shared with designated internal and external stakeholders.

requirements.name: RS.MI-01 — Incidents contained
requirements.description: Incidents are contained.

requirements.name: RS.MI-02 — Incidents eradicated
requirements.description: Incidents are eradicated.

requirements.name: RC.RP-01 — Recovery plan executed
requirements.description: The recovery portion of the incident response plan is executed once initiated.

requirements.name: RC.RP-02 — Recovery actions performed
requirements.description: Recovery actions are selected, scoped, prioritized, and performed.

requirements.name: RC.RP-03 — Backup integrity verified
requirements.description: The integrity of backups and other restoration assets is verified before use.

requirements.name: RC.RP-04 — Post-incident norms set
requirements.description: Critical mission functions and risk management inform new post-incident operational norms.

requirements.name: RC.RP-05 — Restored assets verified
requirements.description: Restored asset integrity is verified, services are restored, and normal status is confirmed.

requirements.name: RC.RP-06 — Recovery completion declared
requirements.description: The end of recovery is declared per criteria and incident documentation is completed.

requirements.name: RC.CO-03 — Recovery progress communicated
requirements.description: Recovery activities and restoration progress are communicated to designated stakeholders.

requirements.name: RC.CO-04 — Public updates shared
requirements.description: Public updates on incident recovery are shared using approved methods and messaging.
